headline: Transcript: Predicting 2026 — Will Ukraine finally get a peace deal?
summary: Sonja Hutson talks to Ben Hall
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the FT News Briefing podcast episode: ‘Predicting 2026 — Will Ukraine finally get a peace deal?’

Sonja HutsonGood morning from the Financial Times. Today is Tuesday, December 30th. I’m Sonja Hutson, and this is your FT News Briefing.

The US has offered Ukraine a 15-year security guarantee as part of a peace deal, but Kyiv wants one that lasts as long as 50 years to deter Russia from invading in the future. This is all according to statements from Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy yesterday. He met with US President Donald Trump on Sunday. Now, what exactly does a security guarantee involve? Well, according to Kyiv’s proposal, it would mirror Nato’s Article 5 commitment to mutual defence — an attack on one country is considered an attack on all — but there’s a big difference between 15 and 50 years. So for now, a peace deal is still out of reach, which is not what the FT’s Europe editor, Ben Hall, thought would happen in 2025. At the end of last ye
